reviews.review_text:
This was our first time at this caravan park, we stayed there for 3 nights. We were incredibly impressed with the 3 bedroom Villa, the interior was excellent. From the size of everything to the comfortable pillows on the King size bed.
The Villa overlooks a basketball court and a small pump track. Our tween children used both. The track and court weren’t noisy at all and it was great to watch them play from a few metres away from our balcony. The kids loved, loved the indoor heated massive concrete swimming pool with 2 slides. My husband and I went and bought swimmers to join them. We had the pool to ourselves and it was very enjoyable. The location was so close to the Blue Lakes and nature reserves. We have stayed in many cabins in South Australia, this one is the best. Expensive, but worth it when you use all the park facilities. We will be back!
Stayed in caravan at ensuite, excellent park with new facilities.

Don't get my 2 star review wrong, this park is beautiful and has great facilities in an amazing location.  Why i had to mark it down to 2 stars was we were placed on a site in our tent under a large tree, we had reservations on this and moved our tent out from under the tree as far as we could.  However the next day a branch fell on the tent ripping a hole in it and narrowly missing myself and daughter.  We were assured an arborist had checked the tree recently.  They tried patching our tent but due to the heavy rain in the next 2 days we were forced to go stay with family because everything just got wet and we were miserable.  We had no apology, weren't even offered a partial refund even though we had lost a 200 dollar tent (I threw in their bin), and had a narrow miss of my 6 year old daughter getting hit by that branch.  A really bad experience all all round for us.  But yes it is a pretty good park, just don't go near the trees...
Had a great stay at Blue Lake Holiday Park. Great location and the park have heaps of facilities. Service was great as well. Thoroughly enjoyed Mount Gambia as a holiday destination
Perfect stay as always. Which is why we keep coming back.
AMAZING!!!

Had a great stay at Big4 Blue Lake caravan Park at Mount Gambia
Fantastic facilities which are very well maintained
Will definitely stay there again

Beautiful place to stay! Lovely green grass for the powered sites with plenty of space between sites. Pool is a huge hit for all the family, the cafe is a huge hit for the parents, delicious coffee and breakfast. Clean amenities. The park also puts on services like face painting and the wildlife rangers coming through to show some of their creatures that you get to hold and pet. Had a fantastic time. Definitely recommend.

We stayed in caravan & had the next door pad for our family to camp in tent & swag. Great amenities. The pool area is fabulous undercover with two slides such fun! The park put on great entertainment for the Easter weekend to cover parents & children. Thank you

We had a family holiday at this park with one powered site one ensuite site and one spa cabin and had the best time. The park grounds are stunning beautiful landscaped gardens. Our children and young adults had hours of fun in the heated indoor pool and slides. It was school holidays and the activities for the children was brilliant. Highly recommend this park for a holiday stay
5 stars across the board, amenities, staff, pool, grounds.  The small slide doesn't look like much but is the MOST fun!
A VERY SPECIAL PLACE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN OR SINGLE TO COUPLES ALL SEVICES WELL MAINTAINED AND GREAT THOUGHTS PUT INTO PROGRAMMES.

> Great location !
Mount Gambier - Blue Lake Holiday Park (Big4).
We (a couple) stayed 2 nights in a Cabin in March 2026.
Our Cabin was fantastic! Private, large, clean.
The bathroom even had a corner spa bath - a first we have experienced in a cabin!
We could park the car right next to the Cabin.
The park was also well kept, clean. Lots of vegetation and palm trees.
The Manager and service was efficient - thank you.
There is a lot to see and do in Mount Gambier.
Yes, well recommended.
Thank you.

Well we r here for 5 days and I can never fault the park nor staff .they keep the park always looking absolutely amazing. The park management r so welcoming  and go above and beyond the the kids love staying here A big thanku to the wonderful staff that helped and assume till the ambulance came when I fell over great stay and a great holiday  i will be back .
Maybe a light at pump track  but all the activities were good great weather pool was lovely and warm we stayed in lakeside 81
We had a great 2 nights at the park. Nice big sites but next time would request a site away from the Road. We loved the indoor pool and the ability to get a good coffee in the Park.

Husband and I were only there for 1 night before going back to Melbourne but even then, we did enjoy our stay at the BIG4 Blue Lake. We stayed at one of their cabins and it's just the perfect room for us. Staff are friendly. Pretty close to the city centre.
We didn't get to use the swimming pool at the time but I wish we did! We will definitely stay here again.

Great location, ensuite sites are worth considering for privacy (and not having to walk too far to the amenities block).
Staff are friendly and helpful. The coffee at the coffee shop is good too.

We loved our stay at Blue Lake Holiday Park last weekend.

We stayed in an ensuite powered site. The site was large enough for a few swags and a rooftop tent and gazebo bathroom was clean and new.

The camp kitchen was great with inside BBQ though could do with multiple sinks in there instead of all the bench space.

Pool was amazing warm and kids loved it. We definitely will be back next year

This park has it all for kids and families getting away. It is affordable and with amazing activities including the indoor pool with two waterslides, the new modular pump track, jumping pillow and playground, not to mention the great new half basketball court and adjoining Pickleball courts. The ensuite sites were so spacious with my favourite (astro turf) and clean facilities. The park sent out morning reminders about their cafe and what's on to offer. Its a great location about 3 min drive from town centre and a 30 second walk each end of the park to the blue lake crater areas. One side features a 5km path loop around the crater edge and stunning views and gorgeous sunrises, the other a view point with stunning sunsets, best of both worlds. The kookaburras laughing in the trees and the starry skies topped off the evenings. The staff are friendly and the sites are beautifully maintained. This park is so affordable and rivals many other more expensive big 4 parks. It's one of our favourites and we'll be back again for sure.

Couldn’t speak more highly of the staff here in Mt Gambier. We needed to put our journey on hold due to unforeseen circumstances in 2024 and have just been able to make use of this wonderful park. They were so understanding and helpful at all times. I would thoroughly recommend this park.

This is our third visit to Blue Lakes Holiday park. Each time it has been a great experience. The facilities are clean and spacious.
The grounds are fantastic and the park is only a very short walk to the Big Blue lake.
The kids had a great time especially at the indoor pool with two fantastic slides.
When they say it’s an indoor heated pool it really is that.
The tennis courts were a nice attraction and the kids loved that as well.
A great safe park where you are really made to feel welcome by staff.

We absolutely loved our stay. We came for a long weekend and hired an unpowered site and our two young kids (8 and 6) were entertained the whole time. They honestly couldn’t get enough.

The park organised a reptile and animal visit which was fantastic. The kids got to hold snakes and even pat an owl, which they thought was amazing. There was always something happening for them. They also had a lady braiding hair and a face painter, which the kids loved.

We really enjoyed the undercover heated pool with slides, the kids’ games room, and the hot showers were great. The grounds were beautiful, with well maintained grass and a lovely herb garden with fresh tomatoes growing near the community kitchen. Such a nice touch.

It is also dog friendly, which was a big bonus for us.

And the café was wonderful. Starting the morning with a hot choccy for the kids, a great coffee, and a ham and cheese croissant was the perfect way to begin the day.

So many great inclusions and thoughtful touches. We highly recommend. Well done to the whole team. 👏

Was worth the 5 hour drive, we will be back 🤍
One of the best parks we have stayed in, clean, quiet, family friendly, great facilities, so close to attractions of the area.
Great Park! Can’t say anything about the amenities because we stayed in a revamped cabin which was excellent, the park is very well maintained sites are big 👍
Neat tidy and very clean
Amazing location, facilities, service. So beautiful! Will definitely be coming back.
Other caravan parks should use this one as an examplar of how to operate professionally..

Toller Platz, sehr weitläufig, Schwimmbad mit Rutschen sehen sehr neu aus und sind top gepflegt. Campkitchen groß und sauber.
Service bei Fragen ist sehr gut. Kommen sehr gerne wieder.
Friendly staff and helpful

Such a beautiful park with obvious effort put into maintaining the facilities.  We were part of a large group: 5 cabins and 2 ensuite sites. The facilities like the playground and pool were fantastic and the slides in the pool were excellent. I didn’t swim but sat in the pool area watching our family and there were plenty of seats for everyone including banana lounges.  Communal bbqs were clean and easy to use .  Just across the road, magnificent blue lake.  A good walk around plus a few other good walks close by with lookouts etc.  we thoroughly enjoyed our stay and if we ever head back that way wouldn’t hesitate to book there again.   The staff were all friendly and happy to have a chat.  It was very enjoyable.  Thankyou

We had an overnight stop here
Park is very well maintained, lots of green grass and clean amenities
Would stay again

2 nachten verbleven in feb '26. Vriendelijk personeel, mooie plek, camping niet té groot maar met alle voorzieningen.  Wij hadden ensuite plek, dus kan over andere sanitair niets zeggen.  Ensuite was schoon en prima. Wij hadden beton, maar ze zijn er ook met gras
Great park, wide open, good location.
Well maintained, great location to explore the lakes.|Room was clean & well kept.|Great Facilities.
Great park. Indoor pool with a couple of water slides. Kid friendly and only a short drive from town.
Very clean caravan park also nice and green

We had an excellent time during our stay at Blue Lake Holiday Park. Highlights were definitely the playground (very toddler friendly), jumping pillow and the swimming pool with slides. The lawns, gardens and tracks were all well maintained. The staff were always friendly and helpful - the cafe a bonus. Thanks for having us, we will be back.

Stayed an extra night due to the high standards. Unpowered site, felt very included in the layout - given several choices. Amenities great standard, well designed & maintained. Great for children with the indoor pool area & outdoors playground, areas to ride their bikes. Close to the many sights of Mt Gambier- definitely worth the stay.

Staff very friendly and helpful. We were lucky enough to have a new very large ensuite site. So much to do for families especially children at the park which was clean and very well maintained. We ourselves enjoyed the pickleball court. The blue lake is across the road, lots of walking experiences close by otherwise a short drive to many other sites very enjoyable stay highly recommend.
So clean and tidy park .we had a onsuit caravan site it was so clean and roomy

Had to rush down to Mt Gambier the other W/E. The Blue Lake Holiday Park was amazing. The Grandkids had an amazing time there. Swimming pool had the best water slide, plus a jumpy pillow, and playground. AAA for everything.
A lovely family oriented holiday park in a perfect location.  Friendly staff, great facilities ... look no further!

Amazing stay near the Blue Lake, with a beautiful rim walk that is easy on the legs & feet. Further to this, a short drive to 'The Little Blue' Lake for a fantastic swim, on route to Port MacDonnell for an ocean swim & a fresh crayfish tail for lunch. Local knowledge on beaches in Port MacDonnell for winds & ocean currents / rips essential for a safe & enjoyable swim.

Sehr schöner Campingplatz mit reichlich Angebot für Kinder. Der Pool ist ca. 30 Grad warm und schön überdacht und damit geschützt vor der Sonne. Die Campingplätze sind schön groß, auch wenn im Gegensatz zu anderen Plätzen, die wir bisher besucht haben, nicht mit direkten Wasser- und Abwasseranschlüssen verbunden sind. Der Platz liegt hervorragend zum Blue Lake.

Our kids had a great time with a larger group. We stayed in one of the cabins and it was very modern having everything we needed. Afternoon cocktails in the caravan that arrived daily were popular. The kids loved the pool, jumping pillow, scooter / bike track etc. The local eateries are just 5 mins down the road and the Blue Lake is stunning along with many other local natural attractions. We highly recommend.
Fantastic spot

Our family returned for the second straight year so they are doing something right, and it's perfect for kids.
Pool with slides, pump track, pickleball, games room and more.
The only things stopping us from going back would be a change of holiday region.
Nice campsite with all the facilities to spend a good stay. Swimming pool is great with 2 slides. Also it is nearby of the Blue lake and the century tower and plenty of walks.

Smart TV in the rooms would be handy to keep the kids busy. Games room having games you don't have to pay for would be nice aswell. Other than that it's always an enjoyable stay.
The maintenance man is a champion.

We paid for a premium villa and were given a villa near the sprinklers, noisy road and we are the only villa without a designated car spot.
1ply toilet paper doesn’t give much of a premium vibe either. Very disappointed and expect better for the price we paid.
Plumbing is also noisy.
This place is overpriced for what you get.
Great Holiday park great location our ensuite site was great  we loved the pool and water slides.

We had an ensuite caravan site.  The ensuite was 5 star - better than a hotel.  The location of the caravan park is only a few minute walk to the Blue Lake.
The caravan park has a cafe onsite where we had breakfast one day and they also had visiting food fans for dinner.
Would definitely recommend and would go back.  We have stayed there twice already.
The best Big 4 ever! We had the best experience!
Beautiful and convenient place, excellent customer service, Definitely will be staying again with family.
Mt Gambier a beautiful place to stay, it was our first time and we will be back.   The beaches are beautiful, the blue lake amazing with its differing shades of blue on any given day.

We are full time Grandparents to a beautiful 8 year old Grandaughter and spent 5 nights in a 2 bedroom spa Retreat. Only one issue was both bedroom televisions had very limited channels and one remote didn't work at all. Lounge room television was perfect. It might not sound important but as Grandparents we  would of liked a bit of more down time when the Grandaughter went to bed. BUT WOULD DEFINATELY GO AGAIN

My family of four stayed in a Retreat cabin, it was spacious and clean and the deck was wonderful, we ate on on it every evening. The kids absolutely loved the new water slides, and the activities offered during school holidays - animal show, magician - were great. Staff were also very accommodating.

Great place to stop. We had a powered site and a lovely little spot for the night. The park is situated right next to the Blue Lake, which is beautiful. The toilets/ showers were all clean and well maintained. The kids loved the indoor water park and had hours of fun. Great spot for the night.

The cabin, fantastic but I was unable to roll on the grass because their sprinklers were on for 22 hours everyday but I’m trying to cut back on my grass time anyway so it worked out. And as someone who doesn’t like outside (except for my grass time) I think the games room should give me more activities.

Overnighted here at the last minute and had a great site in a friendly comfortable and well located park.  The facilities clean and staff excellent. School holidays meant many children which was at times a little too active and boisterous for two retirees and their dogs but amazing location adjacent to Blue Lake meant very easy to get out for a good walk around the lake. Will definitely return.

Honestly my family and I had the best experience at the blue lake holiday park a prime location of mount gambier in close proximity of all the main attractions!
From the minute we got there our cabin was very nicely clean with a modern feel to everything else the park has to offer, my son had a ball on the pump track every day, with basketball to follow, warmer days saw us in laughter as we slid down the slides in the new indoor pool which was so awesome! Barmy nights with a cocktail van in the park for a few cheeky bevs created such a vibe! Fair to say we will be back and looking forward to it at that!
Kids favourite place to camp they absolutely love the pool and slides cabins are nice and comfortable and has views all the way around the park 😊✌️ till next time

Great family park awesome 🤩 play ground great for kids and swimming pool was amazing very friendly people great value for your money big power sites was great for all of our family members right next to us we look forward to doing it again next year 😀😎👍🇦🇺

We just stayed for 5 nights and can't wait to come back again! We stayed in a basic 5 berth cabin, while it is very simple and a little outdated, it is very well looked after and very clean! The spaces in the park are beautiful and green and well kept. The park is well thought out, and there is something for everyone. All staff we encountered were fabulous. The little cafe was great food and drinks.
Kids loved the pool and slides, new pump track, basketball court, Pickleball court, and the jumpy pillow and playground. So much space to ride scooters/bikes in the park aswell. There were activities on each day we were there, for both the kids and adults ie free face painting, hair braiding for low cost, bush animals event, kids bingo, live music and wine tasting in the evenings for adults ect.
Absolutely worth the $ and long travel for us. Thankyou
Good stay

We spend our Christmas at Blue Lake Holiday Park and had an absolute ball. The park has been designed with families in mind and we were super impressed with the facilities. The waterslides were a huge hit with the kids. We also enjoyed the coffee at the cafe. All the staff were lovely to deal with. We are looking forward to our return trip next Christmas.

We were looking for a caravan park and once we arrived we were so very pleasantly surprised. The caravan park is the best we have been to and the staff and the amenities were spot on. We had an ensuited caravan site which was modern and spotless and a very spacious site for our large van. They are continually adding and updating the park so the kids who stay can really enjoy the heated undercover pool, pickle ball court and the bike track. Plenty of walking tracks with Valley Lake and the incredible Blue Lake at your door step.This park is a must , highly recommend.
Great time, great amenities, kids loved all the activities

Lovely location, just about a 100m walk away from Blue Lake and the rest of Mr Gambier is close by too.
I loved the indoor pool and the water slides, me and my little sister had a blast! Also extremely convenient how close all the amenities were to each other!
But, we were camping there in a tent at site 31, but there was a massive dry patch with no grass there! I get that caravans usually stay at powered sites, but please still try to maintain your grass!
Another issue is the camp kitchen. I understand how it’s a small Big4 Holiday Park, but the kitchen was tiny, with only 1 sink and oven! Also it was extremely crowded around meal times, so it was hard to prepare food and enjoy it in some sort of peace! I highly recommend fixing this issue, and as soon as possible!
But other than that everything was wonderful :)

Totally enjoyed our stay at the holiday park, the whole staff made our time fantastic and the way the park is maintained is a credit to all.

I will highly recommend to all my family and friends

Great location and facilities, only disappointment was original attempt to book was told there was minimum nights stay. This is very difficult when planning a trip with multiple stops, and perhaps why it wasn’t overly busy. Kids loved the indoor pool and waterslide, very handy when we had a rainy day.

We had a lovely 6 days in this park. The park itself is beautifully maintained, and is also surrounded by beautiful scenery. The Sites are nice and spacious, even when the park was full you didn’t feel crammed on top of your neighbours like some parks in peak season. Very well maintained facilities, plenty of toilets and showers - no wait for a shower in the morning. Kids swam for hours in the indoor pool with waterslide. Most importantly the staff were very helpful,  friendly and welcoming. Highly recommend this park!
Great location, plenty of walks to do around blue Lake and area. Great park lots to do

Amazing place to stay.  Great service and even better coffee.  Camp kitchen has minimal. So if you are tenting, you still need to pack your own pots, pans and cooking utensils.  The cafe makes a delicious bacon and egg roll and the indoor pool is heated.  Can't wait to come back.
Great location surrounded by lookouts. Close to town. We had a great spot. Quiet holiday park. Amazing heated pool. Shame the games room was closed during our stay.
A great spot to park up, explore, relax and investigate the blue lake
Cosy caravan park. Facilities are a bit out dated but are very clean.
Un grand camping dans lequel nous avons passé un excellent séjour, merci encore!
One of the best parks I’ve stayed at so much to do for the kids and well maintained absolutely beautiful will be back for sure!

Thank you for your response. I would like to clarify a few key points for accuracy, because the core issue here is still being avoided.

1. Your staff made the initial mistake with the dates — this is the root of everything.
I contacted you multiple times with the correct dates (24/12–27/12). The amendment was still done incorrectly. This is not a matter of “miscommunication”; it was an administrative error on your side.

2. Not a single apology was offered for the mistake.
Instead, the responsibility was pushed back onto me, even though the booking error clearly originated from your staff.

3. I have the notes of full phone records.
The call ended while I was still speaking. Please stop suggesting that I refused to listen or that I “spoke over” your staff — this is simply not true.

4. Is there a time limit for trying to resolve a mistake made by your staff?
I contacted you promptly once I noticed the error, and I repeatedly tried to correct it. Blaming the customer afterwards is extremely unprofessional.

5. Please stop shifting the responsibility.
Your reply does not address the actual issue — the incorrect amendment and the lack of accountability.

Given the nature of this experience, I will be lodging a formal complaint with Fair Trading NSW as well as with the BIG4 corporate office, as I believe customers deserve a more honest and professional standard of service.

I am posting this so future guests can understand exactly what happened.

This has been the most shocking customer service experience I have had in my eight years in Australia. I admit that I initially made a mistake by selecting the wrong arrival date (24/11 instead of 24/12). As soon as I noticed the error, I emailed to correct it and also called your staff. However, during that call, the staff member incorrectly amended my booking to 21/12–27/12.

When I called again after discovering this, I was told the minimum stay was five nights. If someone had informed me of this rule during my first phone call, I would have cancelled the booking on the spot and avoided paying the $79 fee. Due to this miscommunication — which was not solely my fault — I requested either to stay five nights or to have my deposit returned. I then suggested using the payment for the extra two nights as a credit for my next visit, but was again told it was not possible because the booking had been amended.

Although the staff member I spoke to was polite, the situation changed dramatically when I asked to speak with the manager. The manager refused to let me speak, repeated only two options (“stay five nights or lose the $79”), spoke to me in a rude and dismissive manner, and abruptly hung up the phone. No one has acknowledged the error, and no one has responded to my emails. All of this happened simply because your staff misheard “24” as “21” and did not check the email I had already sent.

I have never experienced such unprofessional behaviour — and worse, I felt discriminated against because I do not have a native English accent. This was deeply unfair and extremely disappointing, especially considering I acted immediately to correct my mistake.

After this experience, I will not consider staying with BIG4 for any future camping trips. I will be choosing Discovery Holiday Parks instead. If your English is not perfectly native, there is no point trying to explain anything, because you will only have the policy repeated back to you while your concerns are dismissed — and in the end, the call may simply be hung up on you, ruining even the excitement of planning a Christmas holiday.
とてもひどい経験でした

Spent one night at Blue Lake and had a fabulous stay just a stones throw from the lake itself. After deciding another caravan park in the centre of town did not live up to our expectations, we drove up to Blue Lake and the friendly staff confirmed they had availability despite it not showing online. The park was lovely - we especially enjoyed the pool and waterslides!! Toilet and showers were clean, WiFi worked well and all amenities were easy to locate. Good value for money also, so would definitely recommend.

Excellent park - great location within walking distance to the Blue Lake and an easy drive into town. The park and amenities were very clean. The indoor pool plus play areas for children are brilliant. The layout of the park - sites and cabins - is well thought out.

Absolutely love our stays here we come here every November for a little getaway to relax and enjoy a swim everyday in the heated pool we always get a bungalow which is always clean and tidy and it's quite here the staff are great and the wifi is really good and the park is awesome highly recommend staying here if you visit the region❤️❤️❤️
Lovely spot with an immaculate ensuite and fantastic facilities.  One of the best parks I have stayed in.

We travelled from Adelaide for the weekend and stayed in a 2 bedroom villa. It was amazing! So neat, clean and well presented. Very modern with new facilities.
The park is well kept and beautiful. The kids loved the pool and playground. Great location with the blue lake only minutes away.
Great weekend experience. We will be back. Thank you!
Amenities were great, location was excellent and the staff were lovely.
We loved our stay and will definitely be returning
A great stay over with access to the lake across the road. The choice  of sites were excellent with the option to change. Amenities were exceptional as were the access the rubbish bins.
Great park with excellent facilities. Kids, big and small had a great time. Easy access to walking trails and short drive or walk into town.

A fantastic family-friendly park with welcoming staff. The activities organized in partnership with Australian Bush Buddies were a highlight for the kids. The pool water temperature was perfect for little kids to enjoy time in the pool and the slides were enjoyed by all.

Conveniently located near the Blue Lake and just a short drive from town, it’s the perfect spot for families to relax and enjoy a memorable getaway.
Fantastic park with ample sites and clean amenities. The grounds are well maintained and the park is centrally located a great place to stay.

What a great Holiday Park, it’s located between the lakes area. the Blue Lake was spectacular, on our day it was a dark royal blue. The park was very clean, there were plenty of modern facilities and amenities. Many families with children enjoyed the pool and water slides. Everything was well laid out and designed with comfort and privacy in mind. The ladies at reception were super nice, warm and helpful. Thanks for a great stay.
Lovely big site, nice and green. Kids had a ball on the pool and waterslides, cafe had the best Bacon and Egg McMuffins!
Great family park with good facilities. Staff at the front office are lovely to deal with and so are the staff around the park.
Fantastic amenities, wonderfull staff, excellent coffee and just in the right location for a few days in Mount Gambier… We will be back
Nice spot clean cabins friendly staff lovely grounds. Recommend a stay here
Van de cabins hebben wij geen gebruik gemaakt. Maar gezien  de ensuite die wij hadden bij de camper hadden vermoed ik 5 sterren.
Very nice staff at check-in. Cabin is clean and tidy but very dated. Bed is in need of replacement - Lacks support, very worn. TV is very small, about the same as a desktop PC.

It's really great! Right next to the Blue Lake. You can take a walk along the Blue Lake every morning and evening. The facilities are complete, and the rooms are clean and tidy. Friendly and helpful staff.
The children’s most favorite are the jumping pillow and the indoor heated swimming pool, which has two slides and a shallow area for the little ones.
Highly recommended.
Nice caravan park short drive to town.
I loved this place. I want go back soon to enjoy more.

Absolutely loved this holiday Park, one of the best we have stayed at . Loved our bungalow, loved the heated pool and slides, and the gardens and trees. Park is very clean and staff very friendly . There is also a coffee shop which is great! Amazing location as well next to the blue lake, and a  great reserve .
Our only regret is we only stayed 2 nights, next time we will stay longer

Caravan site was spacious and the matting saved our van getting grass inside. Great cafe
as well. We didn’t have kids but the park had activities for children as it was school holidays
Clean and tidy and amenities are great for kids. Booked for a couple of nights probably the only thing spoiling the trip was the staff. Very offhand and acted like we were an inconvenience
Fantastic family park. Our cabin was very nice, clean and great size for family of 4. Playground and pool excellent for children. Loved the cafe. We all really enjoyed our 3 night stay

A beautiful clean well maintained park to stay in, the 2 bedroom unit was spacious and comfortable, the park includes indoor heated pool, jumping pillow and small playground. Is really busy during school holidays.
A small walk to blue lake look out points and animal conservation park, 4 night stay for family of 4 we had a wonderful stay.

Just got back from a caravan holiday here and had the best time! All the staff were fantastic, the facilities are brilliant especially the indoor pool and water slides and the park had extra activities on for the kids for school holidays, which or 5 year old loved. Highly recommend!
Lovely staff, well maintained, great activities for the kids.
This park is straight across from The Blue Lake.

An amazing holiday with the children. The place was modern and clean with everything we needed. The on-site jump pillow and indoor pool with water slides were a hit with the kids. The Cafe had delicious snack food with coffee to match.
Located right next to Blue Lake made early morning walks simple.
Definitely will be back again.
The top of an old volcano. It’s a very blue freshwater lake

Really great spot!!

Pool was just amazing fun for kids

Units were impeccable

We enjoyed our stay in a retreat cabin with lovely views, load of birds outside our windows, it was a  very clean and well appointed cabin.  Open fire was a bonus as was the spa. Super helpful staff and nice coffee in cafe. Love the proximity to all the walks in the blue  lake region. Very short drive into main two , but I walked which was only about 10 minutes.  Pool was great and waterslide a big hit with the kids
Great place to stay. Close to blue lake to walk around. Clean facilities and the kids loved the pool
酒店地理位置很好，交通方便而且鄰近藍湖，房間衛生清潔，園區內有水上樂園，燒烤爐等服務設施，前台人員服務熱情，很滿意的一次住宿體驗
Did have time to use the park but we saw it was great
Beautiful grounds right next to Mt Gambier’s amazing crater lakes. We got a powered ensuite site which was absolutely fantastic!

Had an issue with booking. The team followed it up and sorted it out.
Thank you.

An excellent Big4 facility. A prime location, clean amenities, plenty  of fun in the pool  or on the jumping pillow. Great Cafe, lovely coffee and some gluten free options, which is important to me being Coeliac. Also our three Cocker Spaniels were welcome. All round, we enjoyed a lovely stay at Mt Gambier

We had ensuite on powered site,first time ,an fantastic idea made that little bit of luxury without breaking the bank, caravan park close enough too every thing without been on top of everyone else xoxo
Underwhelmed by their basic cabin. Outdated. Bedroom so tiny couldn’t move around the bed.
Excellent location

Top place to stay very well  looked after rooms comfy and clean .great service wonderful views.id such a good time with my family .great place for a holiday .highly recommend them kind understanding staff
Loved this place!
超級舒服煮飯工具及設施齊備😌又近藍湖，這小鎮挺好

A home away from home!  I was made to feel like 'family'.
The team were extremely welcoming, the park has fantastic amenities, whether you are tenting, caravaning, or staying in a cabin, everything you need is right there.
Close enough to town and 'adventures'.
A beautiful view of Blue Lake, wonderful sunrises and sunsets.
JUST AWESOME!
Absolutely fantastic place to stay very clean, staff friendly. Always enjoy our stay.

Stayed in cabin 22 on the weekend and the kids bunk beds were disgusting.
Top bunk had ants crawling in it that were coming from the closed window, had several bugs in it over the course of the 2 day stay and in the corner of bed under mattress it had some sort of dead bugs nest.
Middle bunk had cobwebs in top corner.
There was also mouse poo in the cupboard.
This isn’t somewhere you want to sleep very unhygienic!
I emailed the staff and they weren’t interested.
Don’t recommend staying here.
A wonderful place to stay and staff are very welcoming. Beautiful lakes and parks to visit surrounding the caravan park.
Brilliant holiday park, spent hours in the magnificent pool. Can't wait to go back.
Great value, close to everything and well looked after sites and facilities.

Fantastic place to stay.
Great pool facilities and friendly team.
Best part is that it’s Pet Friendly

We had a wonderful stay here! The park is immaculately maintained, really well positioned and laid out with lovely garden beds that are clearly taken care of. From arrival the staff were amazing, so friendly and genuine and during our stay I noticed there were always numerous visible staff members attending to cleaning and maintenance. We are a couple with a 4 and 3 year old and a 9 month old baby and the park was perfect for them. The playground was a big hit and being able to use the heated pool and slides at the start of July school holidays was amazing, the children had so much fun! After we left we received a call to let us know we had left some items in one of the cupboards and the staff happily posted this to us to save us having to drive back which was so kind and truly appreciated.
I would recommend Blue Lake Holiday Park to anyone and was so impressed with the quality and cleanliness of the facilities - way better than an expensive resort we stayed at in Fiji! Thank you for having us 💗

This was our sixth stay at the park, the amenities are great and can't wait to see the completed new units, court and pump track that is currently being built. This park has lots to keep kids entertained and is so close to the centre of town.
A bit expensive. Above average prices.

We love this holiday park. Few years ago we came during summer. This time during winter.  We stayed in our rooftop tent with our dog, so having all the necessities nearby was handy. The kitchen has a stove, kettles, toasters, ovens, BBQ and tables inside and outside. The bathrooms were clean, the showers were amazing. Almost instant hot water with great pressure. They have a laundry room with washing machines and dryers (need $1 coins to operate)
The front office sent out daily messages about what they had on the day which was really cute! They had a potatoes truck one night, and some kids activities another. And the main cafe has great breakfast options (bacon and egg roll and a coffee on a chilly morning was the bomb).
Having our dog come with us everywhere was really great. We love pet friendly spots!

Rude we couldn't make our booking
Asked if we could put the money into big 4 membership
And she refused and tried to say terms and conditions
Sometimes it helps to try resolve something.
Keep our money we wont be back
Great three night stay recently in a two bedroom chalet. Would thoroughly recommend!
Beautifully kept park.  Great pool, particularly for families with water slides.  The staff are welcoming and helpful.  Lovely place

We stayed at the caravan park over the school holidays in July 2025 and have absolutely loved it! The swimming pool has 2 new amazing slides (as seen in website) and the pool water is crystal clear!  The park is clean and allows friendly dogs 🙏🏽

Our kids live the playground which has a bouncy pillow, and the walks. The park is lovely to stay at and feels safe. Mount Gambier has so many lovely places to visit and do.

The local pubs have good food and entertainment.

It’s been such a lovely stay!! We will be back with more of our family and friends next time!!

Beautiful and Relaxing Spot!

Blue Lake Holiday Park was a fantastic place to stay—peaceful, clean, and surrounded by nature. The facilities were excellent, and it’s just a short walk to the stunning Blue Lake. Perfect for families or a quiet getaway. Highly recommended!

Second stay here - first time in a villa! And just as amazing as our first visit. Really friendly reception staff. Beautiful upgrades since we were here last, nice coffee from the cafe. Indoor water slides a hit for the kids even though it was a tad on the cooler side. Lakeside villa spacious and very clean. Definitely will be back. Thank you
Beautiful park to stay at amenities very clean staff are lovely.
Beautiful place and fantastic service and staff
I wish to thank the reception staff , particularly Sam who were very helpful in holding our cabin for extra nights when we needed car repairs and weren't sure if the parts would arrive in time.

Stayed here for two nights with kids in a two bedroom bungalow. We came from Melbourne and was really looking forward to it.

Great location, the cabin was neat and clean, loved the full kitchen bathroom with spa. It was quiet didn't hear any sounds from neighbouring cabins or campers.

The playground and giant pillow the kids loved! The heated pool, I expected to be a little warmer, but it was fine. I expected it would be busy, but it was way too busy for liking (no fault of theirs).

Staff were friendly when we checked in, easy to deal with. Only interacted with them that once.

Location was great, close to everything.

A couple of downsides....and apologies if it is incorrect and I just didn't know how to work it....but the tv didn't appear to be a smart tv. I could chrome cast Netflix for the kids to relax and enjoy at the end of the night. It was free to air tv only. Once th kids were asleep I was forced to watched a movie on my phone. After busy day out and driving around and exploring, we would have loved to all curl up on the couch and watch a family movie together.

Also, it would have been nice to have the private curtains so I could keep some natural light in without our privacy compromised.

Apart from those two things, it was a great place, I'd stay here again in the future.
The ensuite site was amazing and the heated pool was fantastic for the kids on the wet winter days.
Great campsite, excellent cabins and facilities. Highly recommend

Been here on multiple occasions over the last 5+ years perfect overnight spot for us travelling between Adelaide and Melbourne, it has everything we need and especially for the dog, started in a tent now were caravanning it, plenty of walking around the lakes and into town, being a diver this place ticks all the boxes, staff are awesome the grounds are awesome amenities are sensational and weather is always pretty spot on all year round. already planning our next stay. keep up the good work

Recently spent a fantastic 4 days at the Blue Lake Holiday Park. Great location, right next to the lakes. Great scenery and surrounds. The heated indoor pool was awesome, even during cold winter days. The staff were very helpful with any queries we had, and the on-site cafe was definitely handy (and had good coffee).

Will definitely visit again!

Great experience. We stayed here overnight on our way to Melbourne. The kids were so excited for the pool. They are already planning our next visit! The cabin was great and staff were fantastic. Easy to access and close to everything. Definitely recommend staying here.
I stayed for two nights, but I didn't find it to be a good value for the money.

The ants in the room when we got there were terrible. The fridge made such a horrible noise we had to turn it off over night, or got no sleep. You had to pay for everything in the kids game room, except the table tennis, but of course there were adults playing that so the kids couldn't even have a game. We have never had to pay for the game room at any other park. The only good thing was the pool. Just as well for the pool.
Kids an i love the park

First time staying at the Blue lake holiday park couldn't fault the park from the heated swimming pool to the awesome staff on site to the clean cabin it was all awesome will be back again. The only fault no tap and go dryers they have two washing machines with it but dryers coin operated
Another awesome Big4 Holiday Park.
With a new cafe and new heated pool whats not to love about this place. The staff are helpful and friendly. Facilities are clean and tidy. Kids play ground and games room. The big 4 has it all.
Great place. Indoor pool for the kids is awesome. Games room equipment didn't work with tap n pay. Dryers only take coins. Will be back again for sure :)
Excellent place to stay.  The staff are friendly, the facilities excellent & the cabins are spacious, clean & the spa bath was a nice bonus!
Great location and facilities, the team were 2nd to none. Highly recommend
Nicely laid out site, just need plenty of levelling blocks for your caravan at the front if the park.

Very disappointing stay! Seperated from our family,  we were told was a system glitch on the day of arrival then transferred to a different camp site 2 days later that was vaccant from date of arrival. When we raised our concerns with park management all we got was deflect and blamed that it was the way we booked it, even though I provided a joint booking confirmation for 2 sites and have never encountered issue anywhere else we have stayed.
Bathrooms were very unpleasant but apparently got cleaned 3 times a day.
Don't bother trying to enjoy the swimming pool as a staff member is in there yelling at children, luckily didn’t yell at ours but it got to the point we didn't bother going as the kids were to intimidated to play and have fun.
We will not be returning!

We got given a site that wasn't suit to our motorhome, but we went to the office and tell them òur issue and they help us and changed our site wirh no problem.
Very helpful and friendly staff. The place is good too and close to all facilities and shops.
Stayed in a lake side villa one bedroom very good I will stay agian

Close to town, a 5 min drive. Was also only a short walk to Blue Lake. Our accommodation was really great, we had a cabin. Quite spacious, comfortable bed. Had all we needed & more. Staff were very helpful & quite lovely. We appreciated the communication texts they sent us each day.
Good clean amenities and the pool was amazing!! Even had special kids activities planned for the long weekend - native wildlife show was a favourite!

Awesome  park.
Soooo green.
Great pool and water slides.
Jumping pillow... love it here

Wonderful park. We booked an ensuit site and our bathroom was as pretty as in a new hotel room. Very clean. Nice heated pool area.
When traveling mount Gambier with kids I totally recommend Kids spot mount Gambier for some arts and crafts fun.

Lovely well maintained holiday park in a great location.  The staff are friendly and the amenities clean and the unpowered sites are grassy and comfortable. Unfortunately they have just started charging extra for dogs hence the 3 star rating. Whilst we encouraged our four legged friend to get value for her money spent,  she was unable to make use of the hot showers,  camp kitchen,  games room, flushing toilets and swimming pool available at the park.

Fabulous park, so much to see and do. Gardens and grounds are kept immaculate. Staff always gave a wave or a hello when passing. My daughter loved the croissants (a little too much) Indoor waterpark was a winner and so much fun. Only negative is that my daughter (who is 13) was sent away as she didn't have a parent. We were more than happy to take her everytime after this as safety is key. But on checkout morning we decided to have a last swim/slide and around 15 young kids were there without a parent and acting quite unsafe (blocking the slide, hanging at the bottom, pushing in unsafely etc). Just need to make sure rules are consistent. Other than this we will definitely be back.
You can not fault this amazing park. Rooms were a tiny bit outdated but still very comfortable. Pool facilities were a major plus and only a short walk away from Blue Lake.
Great location and the facilities are well maintained. Staff are friendly and helpful. The cockatoos can be a bit noisy at times. The pool and water slides were a hit with the kids!

Beware that there's no compassion or empathy if you need to to make a date change when a major and unexpected life event hits you. I didn't even want a refund, just a single date change. After explaining my circumstance, all I requested was to move my prepaid 1 night stay during April school holidays to a 1 night stay during September school holidays, as I had planned to take my 2 young children on a roadtrip from Melbourne and Adelaide. Mind you, I had pre-booked 6 other accommodation stops for each night of the return road trip and not one of the other 6 accommodation providers hesitated to help me move my April booking to September after explaining my circumstances, except for Blue Lake Holiday Park. After going back and forth, I WAS offered to keep my prepaid booking BUT ONLY if I added another night (which is impossible as I now have my new dates locked in with my other accommodation on either side of the date I wanted with Blue Lake Holiday Park AND that there's also going to be a price hike because it'll be school holidays, and to take that into account (even though my original prepaid booking in April was during the school holidays, too). As a business owner myself, I can't comprehend the lack of compassion here, especially after detailing my personal circumstance and having support from every other accommodation.

Our stay was in a two bedroom cabin among lovely grounds. The staff were friendly and polite. The Blue lake is stunning and Mountain Gambier was surprisingly nice! The shopping area was excellent and the people were very friendly. Got some nice pics as well

Great park, outstanding service.

We did have a slight issue with our first room, but it was handled amazingly.
Will gladly be back

Very friendly staff. Amazing pool/water park. A little pricey. Dog friendly.
Construction/demolition was going on while we were there.
Great spot
Older facilities but a quiet tidy place to stay

We were on a site in a Swag. Little small for what we needed but made it work. Pool was amazing undercover. Think the playground should be under cover too. Staff we all excellent. Cafe, ground staff. Had the sprinklers on cause we went during summer. Left them on a little longer for us to help keep us cool. . Had a platter n wine to celebrate Valentine's day. Will come back n try a room another time.

We usually avoid caravan parks like the plague and prefer to free camp away from the crowds. Despite this we did a last minute trip to Mount Gambier and stayed here.

We had site number 1, a small hard stand with concrete annexe pad.  The ensuite was brand new and awesome, lovely shower and toilet with room to move.  Neighbours are adequately distances away and a small camp kitchen (not the main camp kitchen) just for the 6 or so ensuite sites.

The pool was awesome and our daughter loved it.  The cafe did a great egg an bacon roll for breakfast.  Check in and out were super simple and the while place is well maintained.

We have happily put it on our list of places to visit again.

Fantastic view from top
Good maintenance for water reserve.
Good public facilities
Good walking track
Nice park great facilities

One of the best caravan parks I have ever stayed at. Beautiful location, great staff, awesome indoor pool, soft spongy grass everywhere, big trees and even a free wildlife encounter when bush buddies rocked up. And did I say the location directly opposite blue lake was awesome.
A support worker with me day and night.  Some Won is watching 24 / 7. Hum de ho. And I love my dog and that I all.

We stayed there last week and really enjoyed the stay. Stayed in limestone cabin and was perfect everything we needed. Kids loved the pool area. Only suggestion would be update tennis court surface and basketball ring area and update to games room e.g maybe add pool table rather than skill testers! Once this is done park would be perfect. We enjoyed our stay.

I feel so lucky to have had a spot here! We stayed in an unpowered site in a tent for 2 nights, and it was affordable, fun, and close to everything! Great playground, games room, grassed area, and waterslides for the kids and a Cafe and waterslides for the adults, too! Will definitely stay here again when we come back! Thanks BLHP, it's been real.

Our family had the most amazing experience here after Christmas. Staff were friendly, the pool with water slides was fantastic, kids activities couldn’t have been better a stand out was the wildlife bush buddies the kids were thrilled to hold the crocodile. We can’t wait to be back again for Christmas this year!

We came from Melbourne and stayed for five nights during the Christmas period. I think everything was quite good. The social facilities were very nice and well-maintained. The location is the best; everything is very close. We definitely recommend it.

Thank you team.

NOT WORTH IT .

Oven/stove doesn't work , $300 per night for below average accommodation,  dogs off leashes bothering kids . Non secure fencing around park completely open to general public 24/7 .  No where to hang clothes to dry . Poor

We were really excited for our stay, but unfortunately the check-in experience completely ruined the start of our holiday. We arrived just one hour before the official check-in time, and instead of being understanding or flexible, the young girl at reception was rude and unaccommodating (we heard other families talking about similar experiences on arrival)

She didn’t make any effort to greet us warmly, and her responses were short and dismissive. When we asked if there was any chance we could check in early, (for an unpowered camp site) she seemed annoyed and told us no we had to reverse out the park and wait in the town. The whole exchange felt cold and unwelcoming, and it left us feeling unwelcome and frustrated at the start of what was supposed to be a relaxing getaway.

It's disheartening when a holiday starts off on such a negative note, especially when you’ve been looking forward to it for so long. Customer service is a big part of the experience, and unfortunately, this interaction made the first impression of the park a very unpleasant one. I really hope the management takes this feedback seriously and works on improving their service, because the park itself was good.

Booked an ensuite site. When arrived the site was all concrete so no suitable for an annexe. Found a way to put it up but then when it rained the water flooded the annexe because we were down hill. So far feels like holiday in hell.
Awesome caravan park.

Great all rounder. Fantastic staff, facilities, location and value for family stay. Karina welcomed me very professionally even as I was flustered but even the other team we came across were top notch

Had an ensuite site, renovated nicely. Another nice touch is the tapware upgrades etc in the amenities which bring a modern experience to what is a bit dated otherwise. Having said that, this is one of the best maintained parks I have seen! From cleanliness to maintenance to the input for facilities. We had a great time, kids had a blast on the slides. Definitely one of my highest recommended parks and probably the best Partner Park of a big brand I've found.
We stayed at a lovely unit for two nights,  place looked reasonably new. This is our second time at this park, lots of new developments since last time. Looking good!

Close to town a short walk to a
beautiful lly maintained parklands and the famous Blue Lake.  The sites were well presented and the pool area is fantastic.
Great spot for a caravan park. Staff in the office need to have a look around the park at the sites so they can direct customers to their sites the correct way.

Stayed in a cottage. Very good value. Good place for a long term stay. Good size fridge,modern equipment.  Very clean. Very comfy bed. Towels that actually dry you. Massive rain shower. Huge wood heater. I've stayed in 30 cabins this year. This would be the best. Only the second time I've found ice in the freezer for a cold drink.

Friendly staff with great customer service.
The grounds are fantastic with lots to do with the kids. We will definitely stay again.
Great park unfortunately games room closed so kids disappointed, great caravan sites well grassed.
The cabin landing could be a bit bigger to put a small table/chair ( two room cabin ) and pool could stay open little longer  but other then that great place enjoyed it
Good for a holiday with the kids. There are plenty of things for the kids to do. Cabins are comfortable, and there are plenty of spaces for camping and caravans.

The park itself is beautiful and well maintained. The games room had machines down and the machines that weren’t out of order took money and no refund. Wifi is weak and continuously dropped out. The cafe was a great hit for morning coffee & the pool was amazing. Rooms a bit smelly/mouldy.

Beautiful lake park,family friendly ,kid safe and dogs allowed 😄, this was a very beautiful park in mt Gambier close to shopping, and very beautiful! The grounds keepers and the manager was super nice , good price ,very clean. Shalom 🙏☺️ The Gallehawk family

Beautiful place to getaway from Adelaide for the long weekend. My children loved watching “Where is Karen?” shorts on Instagram to see the place we were staying at.
Our site we towed a caravan to was wonderful with quick access to the clean bathrooms.
Our two children loved the jumping pillow to burn off energy and all of us enjoyed the slides and pool.

Stayed 2 nights in a cabin.
Great park with lots to do my 2 little ones enjoyed there little holiday away from home.(ballarat) Swimming pool was the best with the 2 waterslides everyone was friendly
Lots of things to do around the area which was good.  Only down side was the shower temp in the cabin wouldn't stay at the right temp!
We will definitely be coming back to stay here again. Stayed in a Villa, very clean and modern. Great pool and water slides (not just enjoyed be the kids)
Great little park close to the Big Blue Lake. Very clean park with great amenaties.

WOW if you are planning a stay in the South East then you need to book into Blue Lake Caravan Park. Make sure you book a powered ensuite site - we were on Ens 2. Without a doubt the best ever ensuite site we have stayed at. Luxurious rain shower head with plenty of room for more than one 😉. Plenty of space for towels, toiletries etc, great mirror lighting for shaving or doing hair & make up.
Side note -hubby forgot to bring his tomato sauce and the lovely ladies in reception were happy to provide him with enough for his lunch, free!
Great spot, nice clean & friendly park, so much to see in the area, book in for a week to give yourself time to discover, relax and enjoy.
Had a site here for 3 nights, level with power and water. Good size. Grandchildren loved the facilities, jumping pillow, water park, playground.

Unbeatable location, literally next to the blue lake. You can walk around the blue lake and the other 2 lakes.
The pool and waterslides were fantastic, kids had a ball!
We stayed in the 3 bedrooms cabins and they were very new and the spa was fantastic.

A well maintained and appointed camp ground.
All the amenities you need and a stones throw from the Blue Lake/Mount Gambier tourist area. Plenty of nearby walks and a just minutes from the town center.
We had a smaller cabin as we bought the dog along, the larger cabins look very nice though.

We stayed on a powered site with an ensuite, the ensuite was brand new, very clean, very comfortable. The park had plenty to do for children, the amenities are tidy and clean and plenty of room for everyone.
We hadn’t booked in advance but the staff were super friendly and super helpful.
I would highly recommend and we will be back, with our children so they can use the amazing pool facilities.

Great park with fantastic facilities for both parents and kids.
Well appointed sites
Lake wasn't blue
Just love this place, directly across from Blue lake. It's a dream location

Friendly helpful staff. First night noisy fridge didn’t shut off 😪. Asked maintenance staff to take a look - new,quiet fridge delivered by the afternoon - great service and much appreciated 😴❤️ Very clean and budget suited me and my 🐶 fine.

The Blue Lake Holiday Park staff for so friendly and accommodating. They also make a great coffee! The location was perfect, we enjoyed some beautiful early morning walks. Our kids loved the games room and of course the heated pool and slides. Thank you for a great holiday, we will most certainly be back!
Service offered by the staff was way below par. It was kind of spoiler for our stay at the property. Location was good but staff needs to rebaseline their approach to customers.

Had 2 nights booked but only stayed 1 night because the Cabin we paid $250 per night for so very old, smelt bad & and small. The bed was rock hard and wouldn’t even call it a double. It was tiny.
We decided to finish our holiday early and just go home.
(Note. No refund was given for the night we didn’t stay)
Need to service your rooms more. Box sized family holiday for 300 dollars.

Recently spent a long weekend here with our 7 and 4 year old and can highly recommend. We stayed in a retreat with a beautiful outlook, spa bath and gas fireplace. Accommodation was beautifully clean with soft fluffy towels and comfy beds. The kids loved the indoor pool and water slide!

Our drive- though site was lovely. (Site1)  We we close to the spotless amenities.  Reception AND grounds staff so friendly and helpful. The grounds are  BEAUTIFUL !!! We will definitely be returning.
Great place to stay.

Stayed here for a few nights in a cottage. I booked it online because it looked quite spacious but when we got there it was actually three cottages split up so each one was quite small. Not what I thought. It wasn't too bad. Nice and clean but it was quite dark inside because you couldn't open up the blinds as it was a walking thoroughfare for all the caravaners to go to shop/cafe and everyone could see inside.|It was clean enough but there were nicer areas in the holiday park to stay in if ever we came back to stay.|The reception area was large with a cafe that sold coffee and food all day so that was handy. It is in a great location right near the Blue Lake so that was a plus.

It was all a bit underwhelming. I can't think why anyone would want to spend their holiday in a box with children.
Yes it was clean and warm.
Great experience with family. Would definitely stay again

I didn't have a motel/cabin stay, had my own camper and with just a powered site was great. The amenities were absolutely fantastic, very clean and cosy. Recommend you get one of their bacon and eggs rolls, mind went great with coffee. I'll be back for sure

We spent the weekend in a beautiful 2 bedroom villa. The facilities are a million times better than when we visited a year ago. The new pool is great and kept our kiddies (and us!) entertained all weekend ! My only complaint would be the bedding, a thin mink blanket and a sheet is not enough. Maybe an electric blanket or doona would be great to avoid having to sleep with the heater on otherwise we loved it and would absolutely come back!
In a van. Had a great stay would visit again . Close to everything and great walking area.
Clean and tidy cabin, shower a bit small, and very hard to get the right temperature either bloody hot or cold, needs a plumber. Hot plates on the stove are very mediocre,needs upgrading.

Such a beautifully set out park. Very very clean and manicured. Booking and checking process easy and fast, just what you need after a long day on the road. So convenient, we set up and just walked over the road to see the Blue Lake. Few minutes drive into Town. Definately will recommend!

Great position next to Blue Lake but Sites are a bit confusing to find and there is a sewerage  smell next to the ones near the amenities. They change the access codes every 3 to 4 days so you need to check your text messages. The young girl who did our weekend check in needed a few hours extra sleep and some customer service training. We heard similar comments from several other campers that day. Overall an ok place to set up as base while exploring the area but I'd probably try somewhere different next time I visit.

We had camping there. Great location.
Public kitchen equips with gas and electric stoves.
Two toasters and one microwave, two BBQs.
Just not enough seating areas.
Public bathroom and toilet are clean.
Shower head is big.
Hot water is hot enough, and water pressure is good.
There is jumping pillow and a small play ground.
Indoor pool is good, but be aware of the depth 1.2m.
Saw electric car charging spot, not sure working or not.

We had a great stay at Blue lake holiday Park 💙 . Spent a quality of time with family and celebrate my husband's 50th birthday 🎂.
Beautiful location and view from our room. Our villa was amazing. Very easy access to blue lake ,  town is also close. Heated pool is outstanding 👌. Water in the pool is blue as the sky and clean as crystal,  great fun for kids.
Great park, all grassy sites, perfectly positioned to take a walk around lakes and other interesting places. Lovely staff and clean amenities.

Location Location Location this is the best feature of the park. So close to the Blue and Valley lakes that you can enjoy them every day.
Amenities getting old, however cleaned every day.
Nice van park. Fantastic indoor heated pool with slides. Friendly staff and nice position right beside the Blue lake.

Awesome Staff! Karina was super helpful! 🤩 We just stopped there because we needed our gas bottle refill. We weren't even guest of the holiday parc but she called three stores to find one which does the refill straight away. Great service and thanks again!
Best looked after holiday Park ive seen. Water park was incredible.

The park is in a great location for walking tracks and great views of the blue water lake. The amenities are modern and clean. The surrounding gardens are well cared for. The managers are very helpful.
A lovely park.

Lovely big, well located, caravan park with nice gardens and great kids facilities with indoor pool and playground. We stayed in three bedroom cabin which was clean (for the most part- some dishes from previous tenants were returned to the cupboard dirty) and spacious. Will note that heated pool was rather lukewarm and was hard to stay in for long as an adult- kids had no issue though! Staff, while a little brusk on check in, responded quickly and efficiently to a couple of issues we had. On a whole a great stay though a little on the $$$ side.

Great comms via a text with late check in/ key collection however was super disappointed with the fact that one of the main unpowered camping areas is on a slope. There were about 3 flat spots for a tent/van and the rest (heaps of space) was all not flat at all.
Considering it cost $50, which was the most expensive of the several powered tent sites we stayed at in South Australia on the same trip, and this was unpowered, this was disappointing.

Good location & shower block very clean. Didn't use the rest of the facilities so cannot comment.
This place is the best park we have every been too. Would recommend it to all our friends.  Love to come back.  The heated indoor pool and slides are awesome.
Mens main toilets and shower block reeked of urine all weekend.

Overall, it's a reasonable stay.

The location is great with access to the valley lake and blue lake via foot if you choose. The indoor water park is great, but due to the depth of the pool (1.2m) best for competent swimmers only. The town aquatic centre has a sufficient indoor splash park that was better suited to our age children. The cafe and staff were friendly and accommodating at all times. The park is well presented.

The cabin we had (2 adults, 1 x 6yo & 1 x 3yo) was one of the older ones. It does appear they are systematically phasing these out. These are dated and the furniture needs a bit of TLC but everything did work. They are small, and it wasn't really an option to chill out in the room due to the size and configuration so we found ourselves getting out and about more than desired especially late in the afternoon/ evening. For the price point I felt for that cabin it was overpriced.
Absolutely loved our little weekend away. Comfy cosy cabins and so much stuff for kids to do! Julie in the office is so friendly and helpful. Will definitely be coming back 😁
We just had an excellent holiday at the Blue lake holiday park. Friendly, clean, great coffee and facilities. Definitely recommend.
Great place to stay with a family or with friends
Love our stay here. The kids love their new indoor pool with the big water slides :) Walking distance to Blue Lakes and Valley Lake. Plenty of walking paths and 5 min drive in to town proper.
Easily the best park we’ve been to and we’ve been to a few. Facilities are A1, service A1, cleanliness A1+, Professionalism A1++. Great holiday and booked for next year already.

Lovely caravan park, well cared for grounds, the maintenance/garden crew are super lovely. They have a cafe open, indoor pool and water slides and jumping pillow. It was a great experience and a wonderful representation of Mt Gambier. The only thing I’d mention is maybe the shared kitchen could do with an upgrade to match the rest of the parks aesthetic. Keen to return in the future

Spent 3 days at a powered sites. Beautiful experience. Amenities very clean. Staff were just wonderful and the Blue Lake was amazing. I would recommend this place to  everyone. We will be back definitely ❣️❣️

Absolutely Brilliant place and i will definitely come back again.
The only thing is that Ceiling fans should be in the 3 bedrooms other than that I can't fault it
This place was so fun I had so much fun here the food at the cafe was so good to and the beds where so comfy I had the most fun ever here I would defently recommend

Big Caravan Park, which is located right next to the Blue Lake. |Staff at reception are not very friendly. |We did not have a very nice site as it was very close to the dump and up on a grassy area. Amenities - showers and toilets clean and very well functioning.
Nice place to stay, especially if you have young kids. Indoor pool & slides (heated in winter). Probably a bit pricey if you don't have young kids who use the facilities.

Gebucht wurde für drei Nächte ein Bungalow mit 2 Schlafzimmern.

+ gute Ausgangslage direkt am Blue Lake
+ kostenloser Parkplatz
+ Whirlpool im Bad
