Nicht gefallen · - SAUBERKEIT!!!
ich musste erstmal den Badboden wischen! Überall Haare, alle Ecken nicht geputzt EKELIG!!!
- WLAN funktioniert nicht einwandfrei
- Ausstattung sehr dürftig - jeweils nur 4 Teller, Besteck, Gläser etc.
- es droht eine extra Reinigungsgebühr wenn man Wohnung nicht sauber hinterlässt, es muss alles gespült sein und Abfall muss entsorgt sein
- am 2. Tag unseres Aufenthaltes wurde Spielplatz geschlossen und vom Trambolin wurde Luft entlassen, unser Kleiner war sehr enttäuscht
In a caravan
Nice park, but let down by cramped, cold cabin.

This is the nicest and most beautiful caravan park I’ve personally ever stayed at. The grounds and units are impeccably clean and very well maintained. There is an indoor pool and walking trails that lead to amazing views. The holiday units are very comfortable,spacious and cozy and I really liked the detail of the light above the bathroom mirror when shaving. The staff are very friendly and keep guests informed via SMS with info from stuff about the cafe to power and water outages from works. Only negative I can think of is as a 6’2” guy the shower unit is a bit on the tight side and the free wifi was intermittent.
Overall it was a very pleasant experience and I’m planning on making this a definite yearly destination.
Park was spacious location wad the best you can be. 👌🏼
Great park and location, fantastic pool and slides. Road a bit noisy.
It is lovely place for a family holiday.

So beautiful.  Cabins absolutely gorgeous and spacious. Kitchen facility awesome. Etc etc best accommodation most definitely.
Highly recommend it
Everything was great except the swimming pool was so cold.
Peaceful, attractive, clean BBQ, ... and a disc golf cours!
By far the best place to stay in Mt gambier

My family and I recently had the pleasure of staying at BIG4 Blue Lake Holiday Park in Mount Gambier, and our experience in the Lakeside Villa was nothing short of exceptional.

The Lakeside Villa, our chosen accommodation, was a true luxury haven. The two bedrooms were beautifully appointed, with the main bedroom boasting a comfortable queen-size bed and a stunning designer ensuite bathroom. The second bedroom, equipped with a queen-size bed and a set of single bunks, made it an ideal retreat for a couple with children. The presence of a second bathroom, a well-designed kitchen, and a spacious living area added to the overall comfort and convenience.

The staff at BIG4 Blue Lake Holiday Park deserve special commendation for their outstanding service. Their warmth, friendliness, and eagerness to assist made our stay truly memorable. They were always ready to provide helpful recommendations and ensured that we felt welcomed throughout our time at the park.

The location of the holiday park couldn't have been more perfect. Nestled in the heart of Mount Gambier, it offered easy access to the town's attractions. However, what truly stole the show was the proximity to the Blue Lake. The stunning views of the lake were a constant delight, creating a serene and picturesque backdrop to our stay.

In conclusion, our experience at BIG4 Blue Lake Holiday Park exceeded all expectations. The Lakeside Villa provided a luxurious and comfortable retreat, the staff went above and beyond, the location was ideal, and the views of the Blue Lake were simply breathtaking. We highly recommend this holiday park to anyone seeking a perfect blend of luxury, hospitality, and natural beauty.

Thank you, BIG4 Blue Lake Holiday Park, for making our family getaway truly unforgettable.
Great cabins and plenty to do for kids, young and old.

Great park.  Very spacious and great sites (with concrete slabs and plenty of grass). Our kids loved the pool / slides and used them for hours.  It was also the quietest park at night of any other park we stayed at.  Campers were all very respectful and considerate of others.  We would definitely stay here again!

Awesome place to stay at
Great that it's near Blue Lake
Friendly Staff and plenty of stuff to do
Pool is amazing and bouncy pillow

We stayed in one of the villas and it was great. Plenty of room and a decent lounge room. Loved the clean Webber bbq on the decking that I used a few times and the option to get fresh towels if needed. We didn’t use any shared facilities so I cannot comment on that. Great location and the indoor pool was the winner and main reason we chose that park.

We stayed there during peak period but  it was not full. The place was  too crowded and we had a small area just enough for a gazebo and tent. Our site was so closed to other campers or caravans. The amenities cannot meet the demand. Not enough washing machines/dryers (only 4) and dish washing areas as well as 1 kitchen with only one fridge for so many campers. Please consider having more to meet the demand.

Literally just pulled up women’s toilet floor drowned in water and toilet paper covered the floor and I’m not kidding for 2 days. I’ve never seen anything like it. Cleaners were rude FYI BYO your own bathroom 😏😕

Lock up your Stuff!!! This park is a thief’s delight!! A supermarket for them, with zero security or chance of being caught. They wander through Blue Lake freely taking what they choose.

On our first night of a 3 night stay our kids prized possessions, their mountain bikes were stolen from our caravan site. We’d normally lock them up but one of the kids ended up in hospital the first night after an injury on the jumping pillow. Amongst the chaos we didn’t lock up the bikes and found in the morning they were gone.

After speaking to multiple staff the same line was repeated “it won’t be anyone staying here, it will be kids”.

This led me to believe it had happened before which was soon reinforced by the local police after we reported the theft. The local police stated that it had been a regular occurance at Blue Lake for the previous 3 months and they should be doing more to make people aware.

They have no CCTV to speak of except the main gate. The park backs onto a public golf course giving the public free reign to come and go as they please.

As disappointing as all of this was, the reaction of the staff only made it worse with little to no regard or empathy shown. I stopped past on the way out to give them one last chance to respond. Unfortunately It didn’t get any better.

I spent the following 2 nights after the theft, awake from 2-7am to help my kids sleep.

Given how expensive the park is, a great deal more could (and should) be done to ensure guests safety.

Im sure the park will respond as they did in person, with something along the lines of “all parks have thieves” or something similar. We’ve stayed at many parks, free camps, rec reserves etc & have never felt as unsafe and exposed as we did here.

Staff very friendly and helpful, i had big problem with hot water in shower Lots  water gets wasted before hot water kom, then when you are adjusted it doesn't stay adjusted, Rhodes full of potholes and rough.

Beautiful park and garden great swimming pool but not warm enough to use it November.
Unfortunately we had a ensuite site and was nothing like the picture shown in the website.
We paid extra for ensuite and it was awful.  I do understand they are fixing up the park but it was disappointing to see the ensuite that was renovated was empty while we were there.
Loved the green grass ,good caravan sites , great location. Could benefit from improving the internal roads.

Great location, staff very accommodating and friendly.
Amenities are aged, but very good water pressure and clean
Great spot. Good facilities.

Neat park, new pool, nice landscaping.
Pool not suitable for very young kids.
Staff very very nice. Nicest we’ve ever had.
Location is next to blue lake but you can’t see it from the park.
Big grassy sites, clean facilities, amazing pool, I couldn't not fault this place. We will be back again
It was alright great for families with young kids.Felt like too close to other caravans could spread out a bit more.It was close to the blue lake and was great to walk around it.
Top-class location, and very clean and tidy. Dog friendly too which was great. It would have been better if the cabins were a bit bigger.
So calm place. View is so good near to lake side

First time caravanning to Blue Lake Holiday Park, great experience, and is very roomy for 20ft caravns.
Close to town, amenities are great and well kept. Perfect for kids

Location is the best .Close to many attractions. Facilities very good.
Laundry equipment was not all available.
WiFi worked, but very slow otherwise excellent

What a fabulous caravan park in a fabulous location. Right across the road from the Blue Lake with walking options galore. The staff are friendly and helpful with great amenities and camping areas. Will definitely recommend this place to everyone!

Great place to stay.
Well kept grounds. Clean cabins.
Customer service seemed rushed.
Kids would love it here.

Such a pretty park, with beautiful gardens, very well spread out. We had a powered site, close to amenities. Amenities had new rain showers which were wonderful. Great location across the road from Blue Lake with fabulous walks. So much for kids to do & the new indoor pool park was very popular.
Very well located so can go for a morning walk to the blue lake and in the middle of many attractions. The campsites are not spacious enough. Facilities are bit old but neat and clean
Lovely park but amenities need serious updates
Comfortable
Great place for short holidays
An excellent location, was under renovations while we were there and Im sure the place will only improve.

Great location with easy access. We stayed in the new cabins. They were clean and well sized. The park was nice and quiet as well.

Highly recommend if your looking for a place to stay.

Not all cabins are disabled accessible if that’s a concern.

They are also redoing the pool at the time of writing and looks like it will be a great place when it’s done. We will come back again.
The cabins were clean and spacious. New kitchens, flashy appliances. Had a great trip. Would go there again

Clean, fresh and modern cabins. The daily towel service was the icing on the cake. The WiFi here is very good. A lovely peaceful and tranquil spot within walking distance of Blue Lake. Lovely views from our cabin. I look forward to returning. Staff were friendly and helpful.
We stayed in one of the cabins. It's definitely dated but clean and functional. Location was convenient but it is a drive into town.

Lovely location and views however if you are looking at staying in the pet friendly accommodation, don't. Just to name a few: very outdated cabin, clean to a degree, made for 6 but only just enough room for 3 people and a dog, bathroom facilities need alot of work, infested with ants and kitchen facilities were barely working. I don't normally review like this however I am sick of the worst accommodation for people with pets, most owners (me included) will ensure nothing is damaged. It is also eye opening to see nearly brand new cabins with top facilities available in which children can make more damage than dogs and the daily price was the most we spent on our trip to Adelaide. The park was lovely and open it was just a shame the accommodation is not suitable for people (especially the elderly).
Sorry. Not in the Caravan Park. On the outside and looking in.

Depending on where you are staying in the caravan park, the bungalows at the back of the park and not that good, they have a very small heater that take ages to heat up and they don't have instant hot water.

My review probably doesn't represent the majority of what this camping/vacation site offers, but as someone who visited in a different way to most, I thought it should mention my experience (being a solo bikepacking camper).

There was nothing really wrong with anything, and it was grateful for the ability to wait out a few days of terrible weather in the camp kitchen using the facilities, but it must be said you get little value bringing in a small 2m tent with no vehicle, and no saving anything to do so. I may as well have had an entire site to myself, and I wouldn't have had to live with the night lights that lit up the whole of the open communal grassed/camping area at night - making sleep difficult.

This isn't a problem that is limited to this caravan park, but unfortunately is a fairly common state of affairs. Paying $40 per night for what amounts to a shower and small patch of grass is not great value compared to those who come in cars/big tents/caravans and pay about the same (or even less if multiple people). I just wish there was a lower rate for those who are having less impact on those around them and using less space. I've stayed in places that offer $10-15 for a night once they realise I'm just on a bike with a tent and this is much appreciated.

Often we get in late, have a shower, sleep quickly and quietly and then are gone before you're even up - these fees are too high for that, and only encourages people to sleep elsewhere or use facilities for free, which isn't great for anyone.

I would rethink using this or similar parks unless you can negotiate before-hand. It's a great location, and the staff were friendly, but this is ultimately still an issue that can't be ignored.
